name: security-fundamentals
description: Auto-invoke when reviewing authentication, authorization, input handling, data exposure, or any user-facing code. Enforces OWASP top 10 awareness and security-first thinking.
capabilities: Review authentication and login flows for security gaps
Check authorization and ownership verification in code
Identify injection vulnerabilities in database queries
Evaluate API responses for sensitive data exposure
Verify input validation and sanitization patterns
Apply OWASP Top 10 checklist to any codebase
author: DanielPodolsky
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only checklists, examples, and guidance for security code reviews. All 45 static findings are FALSE POSITIVES - the skill contains no executable code, network calls, filesystem operations, or external command execution. References to dangerous patterns (eval, innerHTML, md5, backticks) are educational examples showing what NOT to do. This skill is safe for publication.
valueStatement: Security vulnerabilities in code lead to data breaches and system compromise. This skill provides systematic checklists and OWASP Top 10 guidance for thorough security reviews during development.
userTitle: Review code security and OWASP compliance
limitations: Does not execute or test actual security controls
Does not scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities
Does not perform dynamic security testing or penetration testing
Requires developer to manually apply recommendations

useCases.title: Standardize Security Reviews
useCases.description: Apply consistent OWASP checklist across all pull requests to ensure no security gaps slip through.

useCases.title: Secure API Endpoints
useCases.description: Review authentication, authorization, and input handling in API code before deployment.

useCases.title: Learn Security Patterns
useCases.description: Use Socratic questions to understand why security controls are necessary and how to implement them.

promptTemplates.title: Quick Auth Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Review this authentication code for OWASP security issues. Check password handling, token security, and session management.

promptTemplates.title: API Security Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this API endpoint for authorization bugs, IDOR vulnerabilities, and sensitive data exposure in responses.

promptTemplates.title: Query Injection Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Check this database code for SQL injection vulnerabilities. Verify all queries use parameterized statements.

promptTemplates.title: Full Security Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Perform a complete security review of this codebase. Apply the OWASP Top 10 checklist and identify all potential vulnerabilities.

bestPractices: Apply the skill to every pull request containing auth, API, or data handling code
Use the Socratic questions to help junior developers understand security principles
Run through the complete OWASP checklist before deploying to production
antiPatterns: Only reviewing security at the end of development instead of continuously
Trusting client-side validation without server-side verification
Returning full database objects instead of filtered API responses

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: Works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code for comprehensive security review workflows.

faq.question: Does this skill scan for known CVEs?
faq.answer: No. This skill reviews code patterns for vulnerabilities. Use dependency scanners for CVE detection.

faq.question: How long does a security review take?
faq.answer: Typical reviews complete in seconds. Complex codebases may require multiple focused review prompts.

faq.question: Can this replace manual security testing?
faq.answer: No. Use this for code review during development. Manual testing and penetration testing remain necessary.

faq.question: Is my code sent externally for review?
faq.answer: No. All review happens locally within your AI tool. No code leaves your environment.

faq.question: How does this compare to SAST tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides human-readable security guidance. SAST tools automate vulnerability detection but lack context.
